description: Recurring failure patterns from pipeline runs — prevent known bugs from returning

entries.title: Backend output truncation causes downstream failures
entries.content: Backend agent (S5) produces 16K+ words, hitting token limits. Truncated files break Designer (S6) and Frontend (S7) because they reference incomplete code. Root cause: no chunking strategy, no file tree contract. Fix: enforce file tree first, chunk by priority (A/B/C), add TRUNCATION_MANIFEST.md as safety net.

entries.title: JWT stored in localStorage — XSS vector
entries.content: Frontend agent stores JWT in localStorage, creating XSS risk. Flagged as P0 by Cyber (S4) and Tech QA (S8). Pattern repeats across runs because Frontend prompt didn't explicitly forbid it. Fix: prompt now mandates httpOnly cookie or auth context.

entries.title: Twilio import TypeError (esModuleInterop)
entries.content: Backend uses `import Twilio from 'twilio'` without esModuleInterop, causing TypeError: Twilio is not a function. Correct: `import * as Twilio from 'twilio'` or enable esModuleInterop in tsconfig. Flagged as P0 compilation error.

entries.title: Roles decorator guard bypass via Reflect.defineMetadata
entries.content: Backend Roles guard uses Reflect.defineMetadata on descriptor.value instead of using NestJS SetMetadata. This causes context.getHandler() to return undefined metadata, silently bypassing RBAC. Identified by Cyber audit (S10, Finding 3).

entries.title: Truncated globals.css causes render failure
entries.content: Designer (S6) output was truncated mid-CSS-value (--space-3 cut off), producing invalid CSS that breaks all downstream rendering. Frontend agent consumed broken CSS without validation. Fix: Designer output contract now requires chunking with globals.css in CHUNK A (first priority).

entries.title: GDPR purge script incomplete
entries.content: Backend DevOps GDPR purge script was truncated mid-function, leaving data deletion incomplete. Compliance risk: 152-FZ and GDPR require full erasure capability. Fix: Backend output contract now prioritizes migrations+auth in Chunk A, ensuring core compliance scripts complete before optional infrastructure.

entries.title: API stage 86% failure rate — prompt too vague
entries.content: S3.2-API (Architect) failed 6 of 7 runs. Root cause: prompt said 'MAX 4000 words' but 32 endpoints need ~5000+. No structured index meant truncation lost all context. No CRUD shorthand meant verbose repetition. Fix: added endpoint index table as first section, CRUD shorthand for standard patterns, increased budget to 5000 words.

entries.title: DATE_TRUNC parameterization crash in analytics queries
entries.content: Backend analytics endpoint uses DATE_TRUNC with user-supplied interval string directly in SQL, causing parameterized query crash. The `$idx` counter arithmetic in dynamic UPDATE queries was also incorrect. Both caught by Tech QA (S8) as P0.

entries.title: QA score inflation — 10/10 despite FAIL verdict
entries.content: QA-Agent (s8) scored 9.3/10 but verdict was FAIL with 5 P0 issues. Score (quality of analysis) != verdict (pass/fail gate). Downstream gates cannot rely on score thresholds alone — FAIL verdict must be explicit enum. Fix: separate score from verdict in QA output contract.

entries.title: Orchestrator zero-score on Agent Hub timeout
entries.content: Orchestrator stages show 0 score when Agent Hub returns no response or timeout. 20% fail rate across 69 runs. No graceful degradation. Fix: add timeout handler with cached fallback + explicit error in structured output.

entries.title: PM-Agent fails on ambiguous scope — needs explicit pre-flight decisions
entries.content: PM-Agent (s2-pm) 38% success rate across 79 runs. When user input lacks architectural decisions, PM enters clarification loop instead of writing stories. Fix: Orchestrator pre-flight must provide explicit decisions (data storage, API endpoints, auth scope) before PM handoff.

entries.title: Research-Agent processes then rejects technical tasks — wasted latency
entries.content: Research-Agent correctly identifies backend-only tasks and rejects them, but still processes 18-71s before rejection. Fix: Orchestrator pre-flight must classify task type (market/research vs technical) before S0, skip Research for technical-only tasks.

entries.title: Architect 39% success rate — context-dependent failures
entries.content: Architect: 69 runs, 6.83 avg score, 39.1% success. When context is vague, architect over-generalizes. Compare: fileplan (100%), prd-gate (100%). Fix: inject existing schema files and type definitions into architect prompt before architecture phase.

entries.title: Designer output scope creep — delivers API client instead of components
entries.content: Designer (S6) scored 8.4 but output was truncated at 156K chars. Delivered token system + API wrapper but actual React components (cards, badges, tier displays) were missing. Root cause: designer allocated tokens to API client (frontend responsibility). Fix: designer prompt now prioritizes component JSX in Chunk A.

entries.title: QA duration anomaly — 444K ms avg (7+ min) in full mode
entries.content: QA-Agent: 18 runs, 67.7K avg tokens, 444K ms avg duration — 10x longer than other agents at similar token count. Likely: sequential AC evaluation + timeout hanging. Fix: QA output contract should chunk ACs into P0→P2 batches with 30s per-batch timeout.

entries.title: Quick mode single-agent pattern — task mislabeling causes failures
entries.content: 26 quick runs often become single-agent tasks (frontend-only or architect-only). When genuinely small task → correct. When mislabeled → single agent fails on multi-stage work. Fix: Orchestrator pre-flight should validate task complexity; reject genuinely-quick classification for multi-concern tasks.

entries.title: backend failed 4x in recent runs
entries.content: Agent backend failed in 4 recent pipeline runs. No error details captured.. Run IDs: 1a3f1cdb-53f9-45d7-92ea-0925506658db, 54cec9a5-6319-40f2-9fef-4a5b08c9b68a, 53f31d99-93eb-48ac-99cd-f9685233a847

entries.title: High pause rate: 44/85 runs paused
entries.content: 52% of recent runs are in paused state. This may indicate checkpoint bottleneck or missing user approval. Consider auto-approve for low-risk stages.

entries.title: No auto changes without approval
entries.content:
Do not write code, edit files, or start processes without the user's explicit approval first.

**Why:** User wants to test the system manually and needs full control over what changes are made and when.

**How to apply:** Always describe the proposed change and wait for "да", "давай", "сделай" or similar confirmation before executing. This applies to all code edits, file writes, and process launches.

entries.title: No threshold lowering without approval
entries.content:
Do NOT lower quality thresholds to make tests pass. Task Done threshold = 7.5 for ALL agents.

**Why:** Lowering thresholds masks real problems. Fix the root cause (truncation, prompt issues, evaluator bugs) instead of accepting low-quality output.

**How to apply:** When a stage fails with score < 7.5:
1. Diagnose WHY the score is low
2. Fix the prompt, model, or infrastructure
3. Never change qualityThreshold without explicit user approval

entries.title: Proactive skill suggestions
entries.content:
Suggest operational skills, automation, and productivity tools proactively — don't wait for the user to ask.

**Why:** User said "если бы ты предложил мне эти опции раньше мы бы продвигались на много быстрей" — suggesting debug-step, tune-prompt, kb-update, cost-analysis etc. would have saved significant development time.

**How to apply:** When building features or workflows, always think about what supporting tools/skills/scripts would accelerate the user's workflow. Propose them without being

entries.title: Use Mission Control systems actively
entries.content:
When working in Mission Control, actively use the systems we built — don't just build them and forget.

**Before pipeline run:**
- Call `POST /api/pipeline/simulate` with the input — show the user the prediction
- If overall probability < 50%, flag bottlenecks and recommend fixes before running

**After pipeline run:**
- Call `POST /api/knowledge-base` to persist new failure/success patterns
- Run `/agent-memory` or manually extract lessons

**When debugging agents:**
- Call `GET /api/pipeline/c

entries.title: Duplicate execution ID in history � React key collision
entries.content: addToHistory in orchestration-store pushes without dedup check. If called twice for same execution (pause + status update), duplicate appears causing React key collision error. Fix: filter by ID before push. Also add idx to key as safety.

entries.title: Pipeline run file missing agent output and stepResults
entries.content: savePipelineRun mapped agents without output field � only saved metadata (id, status, score). stepResults not persisted. UI could not detect code blocks in history. Fix: added output (20K truncated) + error to AgentRunRecord, added stepResults to PipelineRunRecord. Now run files contain full output for file detection and deploy.

entries.title: Resume pipeline shows paused in execution history until completion
entries.content: onUpdate callback in resumeExecution only called setActiveExecution, not addToHistory. History entry stayed as paused during entire resumed run. Fix: (1) immediately set status to running in history on resume start, (2) addToHistory on every onUpdate callback to keep history in sync.

entries.title: High pause rate: 46/88 runs paused
entries.content: 52% of recent runs are in paused state. This may indicate checkpoint bottleneck or missing user approval. Consider auto-approve for low-risk stages.

entries.title: pnpm workspace broken next@16.1.6 symlink � dev servers crash
entries.content: Most projects in apps/ reference next@16.1.6 through pnpm workspace root. The actual binary at node_modules/.pnpm/next@16.1.6.../dist/bin/next does not exist on disk. Dev server manager correctly spawns process but next binary cannot be found. Fix: run pnpm install in root to repair symlinks, or pin next version per-project in package.json.

entries.title: pnpm Windows junction symlinks break next binary � use npm install per project
entries.content: pnpm monorepo on Windows creates broken junction points for next@16 (styled-jsx/package.json missing as directory instead of file). rm -rf + reinstall does not fix. Solution: npm install next@version --install-strategy=nested --no-workspaces in each project directory. This creates real files instead of pnpm junctions.

entries.title: pnpm install breaks other projects � NEVER run pnpm install in monorepo root
entries.content: Running pnpm install (even with --filter) re-creates broken junction symlinks for ALL projects, including previously working ones like MC. After any pnpm install, MC needs: npm install next@15.5.4 --install-strategy=nested --no-workspaces --ignore-scripts. Rule: NEVER run pnpm install in root. Fix individual projects with npm install --nested only. 3 projects permanently broken: jira-integration, cv-creator, uxui-to-dev.

entries.title: orchestrator failed 5x in recent runs
entries.content: Agent orchestrator failed in 5 recent pipeline runs. No error details captured.. Run IDs: 444160fb-6dfd-4d16-92e2-ac305e8b99d5, 29d1d8c5-fc4c-425d-86bc-530656300a5b, 16c0be7d-2e8c-4455-809a-3efc5f3efa0b

entries.title: High pause rate: 31/44 runs paused
entries.content: 70% of recent runs are in paused state. This may indicate checkpoint bottleneck or missing user approval. Consider auto-approve for low-risk stages.

entries.title: Pipeline agent corrupted file by inserting code inside import block
entries.content: Frontend agent tried to add CURRENCY_SYMBOLS object but edit_file old_string matched incorrectly � inserted new code between import line 4 and import line 5, splitting an import statement. Result: unterminated string constant. Root cause: agent tried to rewrite 1646 lines in one edit. Fix: (1) Speed Governor already limits to 250 diff lines but agent circumvented by using create_file pattern. (2) Need post-edit syntax validation. (3) Agent should make small targeted edits not full file rewrites.

entries.title: High pause rate: 29/40 runs paused

entries.title: Decision framework — think before doing
entries.content:
## Before ANY action that changes files, installs packages, or modifies system state:

### 30-Second Check (MANDATORY)
1. **KB search** — `curl /api/knowledge-base?q={topic}` — is this problem already known?
2. **Risk question** — "what breaks if this fails?" If answer includes "other projects" or "MC itself" → STOP, think more
3. **Simplest solution** — what's the ONE LINE that might work? Try that FIRST
4. **Reversibility** — can I undo this? If no → ask user before proceeding

### Before pack

entries.title: Simple solution first — always
entries.content:
Before any fix attempt, ask: "what's the simplest thing that could work?"

Examples from session 2026-03-25:
- Problem: dev servers don't start
- Complex attempts: pnpm install, store prune, copy binaries, npm install --nested
- Simple solution: `node_modules/.bin/next dev -p 3013`

- Problem: npm install fails in workspace
- Complex attempts: --ignore-scripts, --legacy-peer-deps, copy from other project
- Simple solution: `mv root/package.json root/package.json.bak` → npm install → restore

Pat

entries.title: Use agents for complex multi-step tasks
entries.content:
When a task has more than 3 sequential steps or involves multiple projects/files:

**DO:** Launch a general-purpose Agent with a clear, complete task description
**DON'T:** Try to do it step-by-step manually

Evidence from session 2026-03-25:
- Manual: 20+ attempts, 3+ hours, broke working projects
- Agent: 1 launch, 67 tool calls, 16 minutes, all 14 projects fixed

Agent works better because:
1. It doesn't get frustrated or rush
2. It follows the same logic for each project (no shortcuts)
3. It

entries.title: Propose new skills when gaps found
entries.content:
When I make a fix, build a feature, or investigate a failure — check if a reusable skill pattern emerges.

**When to propose:**
- Fixed a bug that could recur → propose a prevention/detection skill
- Built a feature that follows a repeatable pattern → propose a builder skill
- Investigation revealed a gap → propose a diagnostic skill
- Tuning improved results → propose a calibration skill

**How to propose:**
- Name: short, action-oriented (verb-noun)
- What it does: 1 sentence
- When to use: sp

entries.title: All tasks must go through MC system — no direct execution without system approval
entries.content:
ALL tasks must go through Mission Control's own systems before execution. No direct coding.

**Why:** Michael explicitly said "с этого момента все задачи через систему, ты можешь на прямую сделать задачу только если получил подтверждение от системы". Direct changes bypass the intelligence layer we built (KB, simulation, contracts, confidence scoring).

**How to apply:**
1. Take user's task input
2. Run through /api/command (intent classifier)
3. If direct → system executes via /api/ai/execute wi

entries.title: No shortcuts — thoroughness over speed
entries.content:
Never rush implementation. Shortcuts cause regressions that cost more time than doing it right.

**Why:** Multiple times Claude took shortcuts (iframe wrapper instead of proper React integration, skipping existing systems, not reading full context) which resulted in broken features that needed to be reverted or reworked. Each shortcut cost 2-3x more time than doing it properly.

**How to apply:**
1. Before ANY implementation: read all related existing code, understand how it integrates
2. Use ex

entries.title: Direct execution spec — full power agent, not a weak proxy
entries.content:
Direct execution must be as capable as Claude Code itself. Not a weak proxy.

**Spec (approved 2026-04-03):**
- Context: full project + history (not one-shot)
- Tools: ALL available tools
- Max turns: no limit
- File reading: 2000 lines (not 200)
- Context window: 200K tokens (not 25K)
- Model: smart router (opus for complex, sonnet for simple)
- History: use execution history for context — otherwise what's the point?

**Why:** Direct tasks were failing because agent had 200-line read limit, 25K

entries.title: Proactively suggest skills to Michael
entries.content:
Michael asked to be reminded about skills and given tips on when to use them.

**How to apply:**
- When Michael asks about system status → suggest `/status` or `/health`
- When starting a new task → suggest `/run <task>`
- When a task fails → suggest `/rerun latest`
- Before pushing code → suggest `/deploy-check`
- When discussing costs → suggest `/costs`
- When looking for patterns → suggest `/kb-search <topic>`
- At end of session → suggest `/session-report`
- When planning → suggest `/mc-road

entries.title: Suggest improvements instead of asking "what next?"
entries.content:
Instead of asking "What's next?" — suggest 2-3 specific improvements based on current system state.

**Why:** Michael wants Claude to be proactive, not passive. "What next?" puts the burden on him. Suggestions show understanding of the system and save time.

**How to apply:**
- After completing a task, analyze current system state
- Suggest 2-3 concrete improvements ranked by impact
- Base suggestions on: health alerts, recent failures, missing features, user patterns
- Use data: nightly logs, p

entries.title: Track and display API costs for every task execution
entries.content:
Every time a task runs through MC API (/api/command or /api/ai/execute), calculate and show the cost.

**How to apply:**
1. After each API call, extract token usage from response
2. Calculate cost: input_tokens/1M × $3 + output_tokens/1M × $15 (sonnet), or × $15/$75 (opus)
3. Display: "Cost: $X.XX | Balance: $XX.XX remaining"
4. Update balance: PATCH /api/costs/real with { updateBalance: { provider: "anthropic", balance: newBalance } }
5. If remaining < $3.00 → show warning: "⚠ LOW BALANCE: $X.X

entries.title: Stopped/paused pipeline runs pollute agent analytics with low scores
entries.content: When a pipeline is stopped/paused/interrupted, analytics POST still fires (pipeline-executor.ts:1352). Partial runs with low agent scores get counted in running averages, dragging successRate below 70%% and making agents appear BUSY permanently. Fix: skip agentStats update for stopped/paused/interrupted runs in updateAnalytics(). Added recalculateAnalytics() to rebuild from all saved runs excluding non-terminal statuses.

entries.title: SWR without refreshInterval causes stale agent status on Home page
entries.content: useSWR for /api/agents/performance had revalidateOnFocus: false and no refreshInterval. After pipeline stop, cached performance data was never refreshed. Agent status badges froze in BUSY state indefinitely. Fix: add refreshInterval: 30000 to match health data polling. Lesson: all dashboard SWR hooks that display real-time status need periodic refresh, not just initial fetch.
